key_insight: A hierarchical Transformer with test-time adaptation can better predict longitudinal dementia progression when important biomarkers are missing at external test sites.
clinical_relevance: This approach may improve the reliability of longitudinal dementia risk and progression forecasting across hospitals or cohorts that do not collect the same biomarkers.
method: Stage 1 learns biomarker-specific temporal patterns from irregular longitudinal observations without imputation.
Stage 2 fuses biomarker streams with cross-feature attention and simulated modality dropout to handle systemic missingness.
A lightweight test-time adaptation step calibrates predictions for each individual at inference.
evidence_signal: Trained on ADNI and tested on three external cohorts (2,316 participants, 13,205 timepoints), ProFuse-TTA was best in 8 of 9 cross-dataset settings and ranked first in 14 of 15 ablation scenarios.
watch_item: Performance was validated under controlled experimental missingness and external cohorts, but real-world deployment still needs careful checking for site-specific data quality, workflow fit, and robustness to unseen missingness patterns.
paper_title: Robust Longitudinal Dementia Prediction under Systemic Missingness via Hierarchical Fusion and Test-Time Adaptation
